subject: english
learning_objectives: Define noun phrases and noun clauses

sections.heading: Noun Phrase
sections.content: A noun phrase is a phrase in which the main word, or head word, is a noun or pronoun. Examples include: the big ugly girl, a fat bank account, the man outside, and the man from the moon.

sections.heading: Functions of Noun Phrase
sections.content: A noun phrase can function as the subject of a sentence, the object of a verb, the subject complement, the object complement, or the complement of a preposition. Examples include: The big girl is quarrelsome; The officer praised the students; Our lecturer may become a commissioner; The grateful boy called his friend a saviour; and The governor thought of the brilliant lawyer when choosing his cabinet.

sections.heading: Noun Clause
sections.content: A noun clause is a subordinate clause that performs the functions of a noun.

sections.heading: Functions of Noun Clause
sections.content: A noun clause can function as the subject of a sentence, the object of a verb, the complement of a subject, the complement of an object, or the complement of a preposition. Examples include: What he said is bitter; The cook gave us what we should eat; Honesty is what we want; We call him what he likes; and The prize will go to whoever wins.
